title: stdlib length/filter are non-tail-recursive: SIGILL without diagnostic on ~500k+ lists
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). lib/core/list/query.esk length is (+ 1 (length (cdr lst))) - non-tail - so (length l) on a 500k-element list dies with SIGILL (rc 132) and NO diagnostic; 300k passes. filter has the same shape and dies at 1M. map, reverse, fold-left are tail-safe at 1M. Audit lib/core/list/*.esk for the same non-tail pattern (find/assoc/append/...) and convert to accumulator style.
campaign_payoff: 1M-element lists are inside the v1.2 scale target; core list ops must not be the depth ceiling.

verifications.evidence: list_length_1m.esk still SIGILLs (rc 132) after printing 'OK ' when (length l) recurses on a 1M-element list; non-tail stdlib length. Stress XKNOWN.
